_comment: Node/TypeScript stack permission-allowlist ADDITIONS. The install concierge UNIONS permissions.allow into .claude/settings.json (on top of the base read-only set). Every command here is a safe, everyday dev operation: run/test/build read source and write only regenerable output; ls/why/outdated/audit and the --version entries are read-only introspection; a plain local install writes node_modules from the lockfile. The DANGEROUS variants of the install commands below are NOT waved through — a global install (-g), a forced re-resolve (--force), a node_modules wipe, and any publish stay gated by .claude/hooks/pretooluse-safety-gates.sh (see gate-fragment.sh), which overrides this allowlist. `publish` is deliberately ABSENT here. Wildcard `:*` covers arguments.
